content.text: Perform an adversarial analysis of the changes on this branch compared to current origin. Take into account authoritative documentation regarding oauth, GitHub oidc, openid and any relevant experimental observations and conclusions
I’m tracing the refactor’s stated contract through the changed code and tests, then I’ll validate any protocol-sensitive conclusions against the primary OAuth, OIDC, JOSE, and GitHub documentation.

input.input: const r = await tools.exec_command({cmd:"rg -n \"OIDC trust|verifiedToken|validateSubjectTokenBinding|trust.bound|token exchange|service-contract\" /Users/cysp/.codex/memories/MEMORY.md","workdir":"/Users/cysp/src/cyspbot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":3000}); text(r.output);

content.text:
**Findings**

No spec-fidelity findings. I found no missing or partial requirement, scope creep, or apparently implemented but incorrect behavior.

The refactor preserves authentication ordering and semantics:

- Unverified `iss` only selects code-owned trust material at `authentication.ts:129-146`.
- Issuer/signature verification completes before adapter binding at `authentication.ts:63-91`.
- The adapter receives one `VerifiedOidcToken`, preventing claims from being paired with separately sourced issuer metadata, at `issuer-adapter.ts:8-13`.
- GitHub binding retains non-empty `sub`, numeric `exp`, and optional `azp === cyspbot` checks at `issuer.ts:20-24`.
- Tests cover the verified-token handoff and prevent binding validation after signature, issuer-selection, or verified-issuer failure at `authentication.test.ts:40-119`.

This matches [RFC 8693 Sections 2.1 and 2.2.2](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8693.html#section-2.1), [OIDC Core ID Token validation](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDTokenValidation), [GitHub’s OIDC claim documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/oidc), and [RFC 7517 JWK Set requirements](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7517.html#section-5).

Targeted Vitest execution was blocked by sandbox `EPERM` errors when Wrangler attempted to write under `~/Library/Preferences` and bind `127.0.0.1`; this is an environment limitation, not a source finding.
